resources.string: Sitting to Standing
The patient rises from a standard-height chair with armrests, attempting not to use their hands for support.
4. Able to stand without using hands and stabilizes independently.
3. Able to stand independently using hands.
2. Able to stand using hands after several tries.
1. Needs minimal assistance to stand or stabilize.
0. Needs moderate or maximal assistance to stand.
Standing Unsupported
The patient stands without support, feet shoulder-width apart, for two minutes.
4. Able to stand safely for 2 minutes.
3. Able to stand for 2 minutes with supervision.
2. Able to stand for 30 seconds without support.
1. Needs several tries to stand for 30 seconds without support.
0. Unable to stand for 30 seconds without support.
Sitting Unsupported
The patient sits without back support, arms folded and feet flat on the floor, for two minutes.
4. Able to sit safely for 2 minutes.
3. Able to sit for 2 minutes under supervision.
2. Able to sit for 30 seconds.
1. Able to sit for 10 seconds.
0. Unable to sit without support for 10 seconds.
Standing to Sitting
The patient moves from standing to sitting in a chair with armrests.
4. Sits safely with minimal use of hands.
3. Controls descent by using hands.
2. Uses back of legs against chair to control descent.
1. Sits independently but has uncontrolled descent.
0. Needs assistance to sit down.
Transfers

Two chairs are arranged at approximately 90 degrees for a pivot transfer (one with armrests and one without, or a bed and a chair with armrests). The patient must transfer in both directions, from a surface without armrests to one with armrests and vice versa.
4. Able to transfer safely with minor use of hands.
3. Able to transfer safely with definite need of hands.
2. Able to transfer with verbal cueing and/or supervision.
1. Needs one person to assist.
0. Needs two people to assist or supervise to be safe.
Standing Unsupported with Eyes Closed
The patient stands without support with eyes closed for 10 seconds.
4. Able to stand safely for 10 seconds.
3. Able to stand for 10 seconds with supervision.
2. Able to stand for 3 seconds.
1. Unable to keep eyes closed for 3 seconds but stays steady.
0. Needs help to avoid falling.
Standing Unsupported with Feet Together
The patient stands without support with feet together for 1 minute.
4. Able to stand safely for 1 minute.
3. Able to stand for 1 minute with supervision.
2. Able to stand for 30 seconds.
1. Needs help to attain position but able to stand for 15 seconds with feet together.
0. Unable to maintain the position without assistance.
Reaching Forward with Outstretched Arm While Standing
From standing, the patient reaches forward with an outstretched arm without losing balance.
4. Can reach forward confidently more than 25 cm.
3. Can reach forward more than 12 cm safely.
2. Can reach forward less than 12 cm safely.
1. Reaches forward but needs supervision.
0. Loses balance while trying or requires external support.
Pick Up Object from the Floor
From standing, the patient picks up an object placed in front of their feet.
4. Able to pick up object safely and easily.
3. Able to pick up object but needs supervision.
2. Unable to pick up object but reaches 2-5 cm from object and keeps balance independently.
1. Unable to pick up and needs supervision while trying.
0. Unable to try or needs assistance to keep from losing balance.
Turning to Look Behind over Shoulders
From standing, the patient turns to look behind over both shoulders.
4. Looks behind from both sides with good weight shift.
3. Looks behind one side only; other side shows less weight shift.
2. Turns sideways only but maintains balance.
1. Needs supervision when turning.
0. Needs assistance to keep from losing balance.
Turning 360 Degrees
The patient turns a complete circle in both directions.
4. Able to turn 360 degrees safely in 4 seconds or less.
3. Able to turn 360 degrees safely, but slowly.
2. Able to turn 360 degrees safely one side only.
1. Needs close supervision or verbal cueing.
0. Needs assistance while turning.
Placing Alternate Foot on Step or Stool While Standing
The patient alternately places each foot on a step or stool for eight repetitions.
4. Able to stand independently and safely and complete 8 steps in 20 seconds.
3. Able to stand independently and complete 8 steps in more than 20 seconds.
2. Able to complete 4 steps without aid with supervision.
1. Able to complete more than 2 steps, needs minimal assist.
0. Needs assistance to keep from falling or unable to try.
Standing with One Foot in Front
The patient stands with one foot directly in front of the other for 30 seconds.
4. Able to place foot tandem independently and hold for 30 seconds.
3. Able to place foot ahead of other independently and hold for 30 seconds.
2. Able to take small step independently and hold for 30 seconds.
1. Needs help to step but can hold for 15 seconds.
0. Loses balance while stepping or standing.
Standing on One Leg
The patient stands on one leg for as long as possible.
4. Able to lift leg independently and hold for more than 10 seconds.
3. Able to lift leg independently and hold for 5–10 seconds.
2. Able to lift leg independently and hold for 3 or more seconds.
1. Tries to lift leg, unable to hold 3 seconds but remains standing independently.
0. Unable to try or needs assistance to prevent fall.
